rationale: The citing paper does more than list Yin et al. (2017) as background; it uses that work as a concrete template for measuring and manipulating emotionality. In Study 1a, Lee explicitly adopts Yin et al.'s text-based emotionality operationalization (e.g., RDAL and textual paralanguage cues), and in Studies 2-3 explicitly states that the emotionality manipulations are borrowed from Yin et al. The cited paper also appears in the literature framing and summary table, but its strongest role is methodological transfer to a new outcome domain.
relation_description: Lee (2021) applies Yin et al. (2017)'s emotionality measurement and textual-paralanguage manipulation approach in brand social media settings. The citing paper uses these tools to study brand status inferences rather than review helpfulness.
